gaps.description: The BoR (v1.0.0-seed) has all six articles marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. There is no defined scope of enterprise, no prohibited activities, no core identity, no human authority reservation, and no amendment protocol. The MSP states that Arbiter (#190) verifies the BoR at every scope query, and that Nomos (#200) uses it as the first of two compliance gates. With an empty BoR, the Arbiter scope gate is vacuous — it cannot reject any action for exceeding enterprise scope because no scope has been defined. The entire constitutional safety architecture is bypassed.

The MSP Rules section is empty ('The bootstrap seed contains no rules'). The MSP explicitly states: 'The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.' Nomos (#200) evaluates every Action Proposal against MSP + genome constraints as its second compliance gate. With zero rules, Nomos cannot block any action on policy grounds. Combined with the empty BoR (gap-bor-scaffold-001), both compliance gates are vacuous — the DIO has no enforceable governance perimeter. The Senate has not yet exercised its authority via the PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout pipeline to publish any substantive rules.

The Spine state snapshot shows a continuous, synchronous degradation cycle affecting every organ. All 27 organs simultaneously transition ALIVE → DEGRADED (reason: 'missed_pong'), then ~3.5 seconds later all recover DEGRADED → ALIVE (reason: 'pong_recovered'). This cycle repeats at ~30-second intervals. The transition log shows this pattern holding steady across the entire visible window (15:37 through 15:44 UTC). This is not transient — it is systemic. Root cause is likely a Spine health-check interval mismatch or resource contention causing batch pong failures. While individual organs may function between cycles, the constant state oscillation undermines the reliability of the organ supervision system and may mask genuine failures.

The Radiant stats block shows the Dream scheduled system is explicitly disabled ('scheduled: disabled'), health status is 'never_run', and last_dream is null. The MSP framework depends on DIO organs maintaining their operational loops. Radiant's Dream system is responsible for context freshness (TTL management, pruning expired blocks) and memory consolidation (merging duplicates, building consolidated knowledge). Without autonomous Dream cycles, Radiant context accumulates stale entries, memory fragments into duplicates, and the DIO's knowledge base degrades. The recent backup log from May 3 shows Radiant context at 184K entries — a system of this scale requires automated maintenance that is not running.

The world state context shows a pattern of SafeVault backup degradation spanning 7 reported runs (April 27 — May 3): two outright failures (April 30 script bug with unbound variable, May 3 hung_then_killed with zombie processes), three completed_with_errors due to NAS mount failures (April 27, 28, May 1), one partial success with S3-only (April 4), and only one clean success (March 28). The May 3 run is particularly severe: two zombie backup processes from the previous day (~25h old) were still alive and contending for the same database, the current process was killed, and only Layer 1 partially completed. No S3 uploads or NAS sync occurred. This is a data durability failure for the DIO's operational state.

The MSP §Genome References section states: 'Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' The seed lists zero genome documents. Without genome documents, every organ operates without risk-tier-specific constraints. The MSP's layered governance model (MSP rules + genome rules per organ) collapses to a single empty layer (gap-msp-empty-rules-002). For example, Glia (autoheal) has no risk-tier constraints defining what severity of failure warrants automated remediation vs. human escalation. Nomos cannot enforce organ-specific policies because none have been defined.

The hippocampus section of the world state shows an empty recent_conversations array. Hippocampus is one of the five Monad legs (Radiant, Graphheight, Minder, Hippocampus, Soul) and is responsible for conversation memory. An empty hippocampus means no inter-session conversational continuity is being preserved. While this may reflect that no interactive sessions have occurred in the recent window, if Hippocampus is running (Spine shows it alive/cycling), it should have some stored state. This could indicate a write-path failure, a misconfiguration, or that the component exists on Spine but has no active data flow.

All 10 recently dispatched goals share identical descriptions ('Routine maintenance goal #N'), reference a gap URN pattern (phase2-low-N-*), and cite 'MSP §routine-operations' which does not exist in the current MSP document. The goals appear to be auto-generated filler from a Cortex gap-filling cycle rather than substantive operational objectives derived from actual MSP rules or BoR-defined enterprise scope. With an empty BoR and empty MSP rules, there are no valid mission references to anchor goals to — the goal dispatcher is operating in a vacuum.

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:strategic-gap:constitutional-vacuum-001",
      "title": "BoR and MSP are both empty scaffolds — DIO operates default-permissive with no constitutional boundaries",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (v1.0.0-seed) has zero defined articles — all six Articles read '[To be defined by human principal]'. The Mission Statement Protocol (v1.0.0-seed) has zero rules — the Rules section is '(none)' and Genome References is '(none at seed)'. Per the MSP's own text: 'The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.' This means Nomos (#200) has no substantive constraints to evaluate at the second compliance gate, and Arbiter (#190) verifies only the hash of an empty document at the first gate. Every Action Proposal passes both gates by default. The constitutional architecture is architecturally in place but substantively empty, creating a governance vacuum in which the DIO has no codified scope of enterprise, no prohibited activities, no core identity, no human authority reservation, and no interpretation rules.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "section": "Articles I–VI",
        "detail": "All six articles state '[To be defined by human principal]'. Article I (Scope of Enterprise), Article II (Prohibited Activities), Article III (Core Identity), Article IV (Human Authority Reservation), Article V (Amendment Protocol), and Article VI (Interpretation Rules) are undefined. The MSP states it is 'strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights' and that 'Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face' — but with an empty BoR, there is no superior constitutional document to subordinate to."
      },
      "criticality": "CRITICAL",
      "urgency": "IMMEDIATE",
      "impact": "Without a populated BoR, the DIO lacks constitutional authorization for its scope of operations. Without MSP rules, Nomos cannot enforce any constraints — the platform operates in effectively ungoverned mode. Both documents require human principal authorship per their own authority clauses (BoR: 'human-principal-only'; MSP: 'Senate-only-write' with scope expansion requiring 'human-only' BoR amendment). This is not a gap the system can close autonomously — it is a blocking dependency on human constitutional authorship.",
      "evidence": [
        "BoR v1.0.0-seed: Status field reads 'Scaffold — awaiting human authorship'",
        "MSP v1.0.0-seed: Rules section reads '(none)', Genome References reads '(none at seed)'",
        "MSP text: 'The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules via the standard PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout flow'",
        "No PEMs have been issued to trigger MSP rule creation — the pipeline for constitutional substance has never been activated"
      ]
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:strategic-gap:dream-cycle-autonomy-failure-002",
      "title": "Dream cycle Phase 2 (memory consolidation) cannot run autonomously — memory engine is dependent on interactive human sessions",
      "description": "The Radiant dream cycle has two phases: Phase 1 (context freshness triage) runs deterministically via bash script scr-bash-radiant-dreamer-phase1.sh and operates correctly. Phase 2 (LLM-driven memory consolidation: semantic clustering, deduplication, merging) repeatedly fails in autonomous scheduled mode because MCP tools (merge_memories, find_similar) are unavailable to scheduled task sessions. The scheduled task falls back to 'direct psql with equivalent logic' but explicitly skips semantic similarity scanning ('no embedding sidecar access'). Additionally, the dream stats block reports: 'scheduled': 'disabled', 'last_dream': null, 'health': 'never_run' — indicating the autonomous dream scheduler is not operational. The watchdog is dormant (alert_count: 0, last_alert_at: null, last_check_at: null). The net effect: memory consolidation only occurs when a human initiates an interactive Claude Code session. Between sessions, context blocks accumulate without promotion to memory, similar memories are not merged, and 27 of 96 memories (28%) lack embeddings entirely.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Purpose (implied operational invariants)",
        "detail": "The MSP states that Nomos evaluates Action Proposals against 'a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules.' The Radiant memory engine is the DIO's primary mechanism for maintaining coherent state awareness — it is the substrate on which any MSP objective would depend for contextual understanding. An MSP with any substantive rules would implicitly require an operational memory engine. The dream cycle's inability to run autonomously undermines the DIO's capacity to maintain the state coherence that any future MSP rules would need to evaluate. Genome documents (referenced in MSP 'Genome References' section) would extend the MSP with 'organ-specific operating rules' — Radiant's genome rules cannot be enforced if the organ itself cannot run autonomously."
      },
      "criticality": "HIGH",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "Memory consolidation degradation means: (1) context blocks that should be promoted to permanent memory are instead pruned on TTL expiry, causing loss of strategically relevant history; (2) duplicate and near-duplicate memories accumulate without merging, inflating the memory store and reducing retrieval precision; (3) 28% of existing memories lack embeddings, making semantic retrieval unreliable for those entries; (4) the dream cycle watchdog is dormant, so no alert is raised when consolidation is skipped. The DIO's strategic awareness decays between interactive sessions, and the system cannot self-detect or self-repair this degradation. This directly undermines any strategic objective that depends on the DIO maintaining a coherent, consolidated memory of platform state over time.",
      "evidence": [
        "Dream Cycle #15 report: 'MCP tools (merge_memories, find_similar) not available in scheduled task session. Consolidation performed via direct psql with equivalent logic. Semantic similarity scan skipped (no embedding sidecar access)'",
        "Dream Cycle #13 report: 'Phase 2 (Memory Consolidation): PENDING. Status: deferred to next interactive Claude Code session. Reason: requires Claude Code for LLM-driven consolidation'",
        "Dream Cycle #12 report: Same deferred-to-interactive pattern",
        "Radiant stats: 'scheduled': 'disabled', 'last_dream': null, 'health': 'never_run'",
        "Radiant stats: 'watchdog': { 'alert_count': 0, 'last_alert_at': null, 'last_check_at': null }",
        "Embedding coverage: 69 with, 27 without — 72% coverage, 28% gap",
        "Memory entity distribution: llm-ops (53) dominates; core DIO organs like graphheight (2), syntra (1), glia (0 visible in stats) have minimal representation"
      ]
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:strategic-gap:goals-citing-nonexistent-msp-003",
      "title": "All recently dispatched goals reference a non-existent MSP section (§routine-operations) and contain no strategic content",
      "description": "The 10 most recently dispatched goals are all generically named 'Routine maintenance goal #N' (N=1..10) and all cite 'MSP §routine-operations' as their mission_ref. However, the MSP v1.0.0-seed has zero sections — the Rules section is explicitly '(none)' and there is no §routine-operations anywhere in the document. The goals are citing a constitutional provision that does not exist. Furthermore, the goal descriptions are identical templated strings with no operational specificity, suggesting an automated goal generator is producing vacuous goals against a nonexistent mission framework. These goals consume dispatch slots that could carry genuine strategic work.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Rules — '(none)'",
        "detail": "The MSP seed contains no rules and no sections. The mission_ref 'MSP §routine-operations' references a section that does not exist in the active MSP version (1.0.0-seed). Per the MSP Amendment Protocol, MSP rules are created only through PEMs issued by Nomos when an AP is blocked — no such PEMs have been issued, so no MSP sections have been created. Goals referencing fabricated MSP sections indicate a fault in the goal-generation pipeline: it is either hallucinating constitutional authority or using a template that predates the seed MSP and was never updated."
      },
      "criticality": "MEDIUM",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "The goal dispatch system is generating placeholder goals that: (1) cite nonexistent constitutional authority, undermining the legal integrity of every dispatched Action Proposal; (2) consume the goal queue with vacuous entries, preventing genuine strategic goals from being dispatched; (3) mask the absence of real strategic direction by creating the appearance of activity. If Nomos were to evaluate these goals against the actual MSP (which has no §routine-operations), they would either fail validation or, worse, pass under default-permissive mode without scrutiny. This gap compounds the constitutional vacuum (Gap #1) — not only are there no MSP rules, but the goal engine is fabricating false references to nonexistent rules.",
      "evidence": [
        "All 10 recent goals have mission_ref: 'MSP §routine-operations'",
        "MSP v1.0.0-seed Rules section: '(none)' — no §routine-operations exists",
        "Goal descriptions are identical template: 'Routine maintenance goal #N' with no operational detail",
        "Goal IDs follow pattern urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-low-{N}-{random} suggesting automated batch generation",
        "gap_ref values cite 'cortex-gap' URNs not visible in the provided Radiant context/memory blocks"
      ]
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:strategic-gap:chronic-infra-drift-ungoverned-004",
      "title": "Chronic infrastructure failures (NAS backups, GitLab sync) persist for weeks without governance escalation or PEM issuance",
      "description": "NAS backup destination has been unavailable since at least April 7, 2026 — recurring in backup results on April 7, 27, 28, 30, and May 1.
